calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2009 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders the balance of this case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings reported by: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. (212)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-08-2008 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-08-2008 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-17-09 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Settlement Conference. Commissioner William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Clarissa Kearns, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Nataliya Lekareva and Emil Rayzberg, who were not present at today's hearing.
Theodore F. Aldrich, Esq. appeared for defendants City & County of San Francisco ("CCSF") and San Francisco Symphony ("SFS").
Brian D. Seibel, Esq. appeared for defendant Golden State Carpet Service, Inc. ("GSCS").
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on September 15, 2008.

Terms of the Settlement Agreement were read into the record by Mr. Aldrich. In accordance with the agreement of the parties, defendants CCSF & SFS shall pay plaintiffs $187,000.00 within thirty days of today's date. Defendant GSCS shall pay defendants $13,000.00 within thirty days of today's date.

Good faith settlement finding was issued by the Court in Department 302 on Friday, December 5, 2008 as to defendant CCSF & SSF. All parties stipulated to a good faith settlement finding made by the Court as to defendant GSCS. This agreement of the parties shall be reduced to writing, and there shall be two separate settlement agreements and releases. (Civil Code Section 1542). Each party shall bear their own fees and costs. Counsel for plaintiffs shall dismiss the Doe Defendants after full payment has been made by defendants CCSF, SFS & CSCS.

Matter is continued to February 17, 2009 @ 10:00 a.m. in Department 212 on the Dismissal Calendar - CRC 3.1385.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement With Pltfs
Off Calendar Dec-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO SYMPHONY AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement With Plaintiffs.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. The settlement is in good faith given the parties' negotiations. Golden Gate has presented no evidence of collusion. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
